description_long: This object is a compact, rectangular handheld electronic device, most likely a professional two-way radio or communication transceiver. The front face features a digital LCD display, several tactile physical buttons labeled with functions such as MENU, SCAN, and LOCK, and a prominent brand marking 'THALES'. The device has a ribbed texture on the main body for grip, and a protruding antenna on one end, indicating its communication purpose. The sides of the device show additional buttons and a secondary display or label area, suggesting advanced functionality. The overall construction appears robust, suitable for field or urban use, with a matte black finish and clear, utilitarian design.
description: A rugged, handheld black communication device with an LCD screen, physical buttons, and a protruding antenna, branded 'THALES'.
description_view_0: Front view showing the antenna end, main LCD display, and primary control buttons.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the antenna, ribbed grip, and side button panel.
description_view_2: Rear view with the main control interface, LCD display, and clear 'THALES' branding.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the antenna, side buttons, and secondary display or label.
